summary: Pristine soundboard capture of a mixed‑bag show highlighted by Jerry’s double “headlight” verse and an excellent Baby Blue.
review: The Miller‑processed soundboard tape is crisp and almost flawless, giving the listener a clean window onto the performance. Musically the concert is uneven – the early set shines with a rare double “headlight” vocal from Jerry and a sweet rendition of Baby Blue, but the second set feels predictable and a rushed “Eyes of the World” drags the show down.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently praised as pristine (high score), while show quality receives mixed feedback ranging from “good” to “humdrum,” pulling the overall rating toward the middle of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, pristine mix with Millerized processing that adds warmth; no drop‑outs or major balance issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early set flows well and carries an Oregon‑cool vibe, but the second half feels formulaic and lacks the spark that earlier shows of ’84 delivered.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Delivers a powerful double “headlight” verse with authority; highlighted as a rare vocal moment.
band_member_comments.Drums: Second‑set drumming is noted as merely a prelude to an obligatory, predictable jam.
